---
title: "Experiment 1 Analyses"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
library(stringr)
library(proxy)

# Load the package
library(osfr)
```

```{r}

analysis_dir<- getwd()

data_dir <-paste0(analysis_dir,'/../data/exp_1')
### if data_dir doesn't exist create it
if (!dir.exists(data_dir)) {
  dir.create(data_dir)
}

results_dir <-paste0(analysis_dir,'/../results/')
### if results_dir doesn't exist create it
if (!dir.exists(results_dir)) {
  dir.create(results_dir)
}

plot_dir <- paste0(analysis_dir,'/../plots/')

### if plot_dir doesn't exist create it
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir)) {
  dir.create(plot_dir)
}



```

### OSF Download if needed

```{r}

osf_token <- readLines("../km_osf_pat.txt", warn = FALSE)[1]
# Authenticate with OSF (you'll be prompted to generate a PAT token)
osf_auth(token=osf_token)

# Retrieve the OSF component
osf_project <- osf_retrieve_node("cty4v")

# List all files in the component
files <- osf_ls_files(osf_project, n_max = Inf)


# Download all files
osf_download(files, path = data_dir, conflicts = "overwrite")

# If you want to download files recursively from subdirectories:
# osf_download(osf_project, path = local_dir, recurse = TRUE, conflicts = "overwrite")

```

### Load data files

```{r}
data_files <-list.files(path = data_dir, full.names = TRUE, pattern="*.csv")
print(paste0('we have data from ',length(data_files),' participants'))



df_list <- lapply(data_files, read.csv)

experiment_data_raw<- bind_rows(df_list)

ratings_trials<- experiment_data_raw %>% filter(trial_type== 'html-slider-response')

ratings_trials_exp<- ratings_trials %>% filter(!practiceTrial %in% c('True','true'))

### filter ratings_trials_exp such that we have only 1 subject_id per condition_num
set.seed(56)  
ratings_trials_exp <- ratings_trials_exp %>%
  group_by(condition_num) %>%
  filter(subject_id %in% sample(unique(subject_id), 1)) %>%
  ungroup()

### every instance of 'gist' in the scale_name column should be replaced with 'gist_earth'
ratings_trials_exp$scale_name <- gsub("gist", "gist_earth", ratings_trials_exp$scale_name)

ratings_trials_exp$response<- as.numeric(ratings_trials_exp$response)
ratings_trials_exp$response<- (ratings_trials_exp$response + 200)/400


### fix string parsing bug for images of gist_earth

ratings_trials_exp <- ratings_trials_exp %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Fix scale_name: If it's "gist", it should be "gist_earth"
    scale_name = if_else(scale_name == "gist", "gist_earth", scale_name),
    
    # 2. Fix mapping_code: If it was "earth", extract it from the path.
    # We look for 'scaleDef' or 'scaleRev' in the image_path.
    mapping_code = if_else(
      mapping_code == "earth", 
      str_extract(image_path, "scaleDef|scaleRev"), 
      mapping_code
    ),
    
    # 3. Fix orientation_code: If it was 'scaleDef' (shifted), 
    # extract 'orientDef' or 'orientMir' from the path.
    orientation_code = if_else(
      scale_name == "gist_earth" & orientation_code == "scaleDef", 
      str_extract(image_path, "orientDef|orientMir"), 
      orientation_code
    ),
    
    # 4. Fix rotate_code: If it was 'orientDef' (shifted), 
    # extract the 3 digits before .png
    rotation_code = if_else(
      scale_name == "gist_earth" & rotation_code == "orientDef", 
      str_extract(image_path, "\\d{3}(?=\\.png)"), 
      rotation_code
    )
  )

ratings_trials_exp_grouped <- ratings_trials_exp%>%group_by(concept,scale_name)%>%summarize(mean_rating = mean(response))


### get the number of unique participants per condition in ratings_trials_exp
subject_dist<-ratings_trials_exp %>% 
  group_by(condition_num) %>% 
  summarize(num_participants = n_distinct(subject_id))

subject_dist

### can you list all the numbers between 0 and 79 not present in subject_dist$condition_num
missing_conditions <- setdiff(0:79, subject_dist$condition_num)
print(paste0('missing conditions: ', paste(missing_conditions, collapse = ', ')))


write.csv(ratings_trials_exp_grouped, '../data/exp1_ratings_trials_exp_grouped.csv')


# 
# length(ratings_trials_exp%>%filter(image_path == "images/map02_viridis_scaleDef_orientMir_000.png")%>%pull(subject_id)%>%unique())
# ratings_trials_exp%>%filter(condition_num==61)%>%pull(image_path)%>%unique()
```

```{r}

predicted_ratings<- read.csv('../results/matplotlib_colorscales_w_predictions_2.csv')

### assuming equal presence of each color
predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform <- predicted_ratings %>% 
  group_by(colorscale) %>% 
  summarize(
    angry = mean(angry_pred),
    fearful = mean(fearful_pred), 
    happy = mean(happy_pred), 
    sad = mean(sad_pred), 
    disgust = mean(disgust_pred)
  )

predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform <- predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform %>%
  rename(scale_name = colorscale)

predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform_long <- predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform%>%pivot_longer(cols=c(angry,fearful,happy,sad,disgust), names_to='concept', values_to='mean_prediction')

### combine predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform_long with ratings_trials_exp_grouped matching on concept and scale_name

analysis_df <- ratings_trials_exp_grouped %>%
  inner_join(predicted_ratings_grouped_uniform_long, by = c("concept", "scale_name"))

```

```{r}

### what is the overall correlation

print(corr.test(analysis_df$mean_rating, analysis_df$mean_prediction))


results <- data.frame(
  emotion = c("angry", "fearful", "happy", "sad", "disgust"),
  correlation = c(
    corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='angry')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='angry')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$r,
    corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='fearful')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='fearful')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$r,
    corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='happy')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='happy')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$r,
    corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='sad')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='sad')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$r,
    corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='disgust')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='disgust')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$r
  ),
  p_value = c(
  corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='angry')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='angry')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$p,
  corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='fearful')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='fearful')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$p,
  corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='happy')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='happy')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$p,
  corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='sad')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='sad')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$p,
  corr.test(analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='disgust')%>%pull(mean_rating), analysis_df%>%filter(concept=='disgust')%>%pull(mean_prediction))$p
  )
)

print(results)
```

```{r}
### make a faceted scatterplot of predicted vs. observed ratings for each emotion (e.g.,  analysis_df$mean_rating vs. analysis_df$mean_prediction))
library(ggimage)

analysis_df$path_name <- paste0("../images/small/map02_", analysis_df$scale_name, "_scaleDef_orientDef_000_25px.png")
# 2. Build the plot
p <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = mean_prediction, y = mean_rating)) +
  geom_image(aes(image = path_name), size = 0.05) + 
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm', se = FALSE, color = '#06c7bd') +
  facet_wrap(~concept, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = 'Predicted Ratings', y = 'Human Ratings') +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  theme(
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2, "lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 10, 10, "pt"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
     strip.text = element_text(size = 12) 
  )


# 3. Save the result
n_facets <- length(unique(analysis_df$concept))
panel_size <- 3.5  # inches per panel
ggsave(
  paste0(plot_dir, 'exp_1_scatterplot_predicted_vs_observed_ratings.pdf'), 
  plot = p, 
  width = panel_size * n_facets + 1,  # +1 for margins/labels
  height = panel_size + 1.5,           # +1.5 for axis labels/title
  dpi = 300
)

p
```

### Subsetting scales for future experiments (40 out of 80)

```{r}
ratings_trials_exp_scale_mapping_grouped<- ratings_trials_exp%>%group_by(concept, scale_name)%>%summarize(mean_rating = mean(response))%>%ungroup()


ratings_trials_exp_scale_mapping_grouped%>%
  group_by(concept) %>%
  summarize(n = n())


map_x_concept_df <- ratings_trials_exp_scale_mapping_grouped %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(scale_name), #  These stay as rows
    names_from = concept,                  # These values become column headers
    values_from = mean_rating,             # These values fill the cells
    names_prefix = "rating_"               # Optional: adds a prefix to make names meaningful
  )



```

#### Search to find the most dissimilar rows

```{r}
library(proxy) # For distance matrices

# 1. Prepare the data: Extract only the 5 numeric rating columns
# Let's assume your wide df is named 'df_wide'
feature_matrix <- map_x_concept_df %>% 
  select(starts_with("rating_")) %>% 
  as.matrix()

# 2. Compute the distance matrix (Euclidean)
dist_matrix <- as.matrix(dist(feature_matrix, method = "euclidean"))

# 3. Greedy Selection Algorithm
n_to_select <- 20
n_total <- nrow(map_x_concept_df)

# Start by picking the two furthest points
initial_pair <- which(dist_matrix == max(dist_matrix), arr.ind = TRUE)[1, ]
selected_indices <- as.vector(initial_pair)

# Iteratively add the point that is furthest from the current set
while(length(selected_indices) < n_to_select) {
  # For all points NOT yet selected...
  remaining_indices <- setdiff(1:n_total, selected_indices)
  
  # Calculate the distance from each remaining point to its NEAREST neighbor in the selected set
  # We want the point whose "nearest neighbor" is as far away as possible
  min_dists <- apply(dist_matrix[remaining_indices, selected_indices, drop = FALSE], 1, min)
  
  # Pick the index that maximizes that minimum distance
  next_index <- remaining_indices[which.max(min_dists)]
  selected_indices <- c(selected_indices, next_index)
}

# 4. Filter the original wide dataframe
diverse_set <- map_x_concept_df[selected_indices, ]

# Quick Check
print(nrow(diverse_set))
head(diverse_set)


```

## Generating Shifted Maps

```{r}
library(data.table)
color_defs <- fread("../data/matplotlib_colorscales.csv")

# Function to convert RGB (0-1) to Hex
rgb_to_hex <- function(r, g, b) {
  rgb(r, g, b, maxColorValue = 1)
}


# Rotate matrix 90 degrees clockwise
clockwise90 <- function(m) t(apply(m, 2, rev))

# Mirror matrix (Left-Right flip)
mirror_matrix <- function(m) m[, rev(seq_len(ncol(m)))]


# 2. Setup Base Output Directory
base_out_dir <- "../images/shifted_maps/"
dir.create(base_out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
# Target map IDs
target_ids <- c(23, 7, 15, 2, 27, 26, 5, 10, 13, 12)

# Outer Loop: Shift Type
for(shift in c('darkshift', 'lightshift')) {
  
  inp_csv_path <- paste0("../data/map_values_V2_", shift, ".csv")
  if (!file.exists(inp_csv_path)) next
  
  map_data <- fread(file = inp_csv_path, header = TRUE, data.table = FALSE)

  # Loop through 20 Scales
  for (s in 1:nrow(diverse_set)) {
    
    current_scale_name <- diverse_set$scale_name[s]
    
    # Prepare both color vectors (Default and Reversed)
    scale_colors <- color_defs %>%
      filter(colorscale == current_scale_name) %>%
      arrange(index) %>%
      mutate(hex = rgb_to_hex(R, G, B))
    
    col_def <- scale_colors$hex
    col_rev <- rev(col_def)
    
    cat(paste0("\nProcessing ", shift, " for scale: ", current_scale_name, "\n"))

    # Loop through Maps
    for (i in 1:nrow(map_data)) {
      
      
      map_id_num <- as.numeric(map_data[i, 1])
      if(!(map_id_num %in% target_ids)) next
      
      # Step A: Create the base aggregated matrix
      v <- as.numeric(map_data[i, -1])
      m_base <- matrix(data = v, nrow = 1000, ncol = 1000)
      m_250 <- apply(array(m_base, c(4, 250, 4, 250)), c(2, 4), mean)
      
      # Step B: Generate spatial orientations
      # Define as list: Name = Matrix
      orientations <- list(
        "orientDef_000" = m_250,
        "orientDef_090" = clockwise90(m_250),
        "orientDef_180" = clockwise90(clockwise90(m_250)),
        "orientDef_270" = clockwise90(clockwise90(clockwise90(m_250)))
      )
      
      # Add mirrored versions
      m_mirror <- mirror_matrix(m_250)
      orientations[["orientMir_000"]] <- m_mirror
      orientations[["orientMir_090"]] <- clockwise90(m_mirror)
      orientations[["orientMir_180"]] <- clockwise90(clockwise90(m_mirror))
      orientations[["orientMir_270"]] <- clockwise90(clockwise90(clockwise90(m_mirror)))

      # Step C: Save out all combinations
      for (o_name in names(orientations)) {
        
        # 1. Define filenames first
        fname_def <- sprintf("map%02d_%s_scaleDef_%s_%s.png", map_id_num, current_scale_name, o_name, shift)
        fname_rev <- sprintf("map%02d_%s_scaleRev_%s_%s.png", map_id_num, current_scale_name, o_name, shift)
        
        path_def <- file.path(base_out_dir, fname_def)
        path_rev <- file.path(base_out_dir, fname_rev)

        # 2. Check if BOTH exist to potentially skip matrix transformation
        if (file.exists(path_def) && file.exists(path_rev)) {
          next # Skip to the next orientation
        }

        # 3. Only perform the heavy matrix flip if we actually need to save a file
        m_to_plot <- t(apply(orientations[[o_name]], 1, rev)) 
        
        # 4. Save ScaleDef version if it doesn't exist
        if (!file.exists(path_def)) {
          png(path_def, width = 500, height = 500, type = "cairo", antialias = "none")
          par(mar = c(0,0,0,0))
          image(m_to_plot, axes = FALSE, asp = 1, useRaster = TRUE, col = col_def)
          dev.off()
        }
        
        # 5. Save ScaleRev version if it doesn't exist
        if (!file.exists(path_rev)) {
          png(path_rev, width = 500, height = 500, type = "cairo", antialias = "none")
          par(mar = c(0,0,0,0))
          image(m_to_plot, axes = FALSE, asp = 1, useRaster = TRUE, col = col_rev)
          dev.off()
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```
